subject: Public Health
title: Zoonotic Disease Spillover
description: Learn Zoonotic Disease Spillover in one minute.

hook.text: 75% of emerging infectious diseases jump from animals to humans—the next pandemic is brewing in wildlife.

buildup.text: Deforestation, wildlife trade, and factory farming bring humans and animal pathogens into closer contact.

discovery.text: HIV came from chimpanzees. Ebola from bats. SARS from civets. COVID-19 likely from wildlife too.

twist.text: Preventing spillover is cheaper than responding to pandemics—forest protection costs billions less than COVID-19 did.

climax.text: One Health—linking human, animal, and environmental health—is essential for preventing future pandemics.

punchline.text: Destroying habitats doesn't just kill animals. It unleashes their viruses on us.

quiz.question: What percentage of emerging infectious diseases come from animals?
